mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower cost of capital directly increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.98% as of Feb 2008 reflects Fed rate cuts in response to credit crisis
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. Elevated unemployment predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% in Feb 2008, near cyclical peak before financial crisis acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite UNRATE rising above threshold, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales signal weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth. Lower retail sales activity predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380197 million in Feb 2008, showing early signs of consumer weakness
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 60% of instances when RSAFS declines below trigger threshold, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. Sustained decline in industrial production signals supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.23 in Jan 2008, beginning to decelerate amid credit crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of months when INDPRO growth is negative, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and payroll employment jointly signal household formation and income growth. When both HOUST and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, it indicates a demand shock affecting both residential investment and labor income, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 2% and PAYEMS growth turns negative month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1012 thousand in Jan 2008, declining sharply; PAYEMS at 137993 thousand in Feb 2008, showing employment weakness
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock in housing and labor markets
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 65% of triggered instances despite joint HOUST and PAYEMS weakness, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI and monetary policy stance measured by FEDFUNDS interact to determine real interest rates. When CPI inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains low or declining, real rates compress, but if inflation accelerates faster than Fed easing, real rates rise, signaling monetary tightening that reduces equity valuations and produces negative returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.571 in Feb 2008, reflecting energy and commodity inflation; FEDFUNDS at 2.98% showing aggressive easing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) has Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth reflects labor market momentum and corporate hiring confidence. Sustained payroll growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as strong employment data signals robust earnings growth and reduces recession risk premium in equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137993 thousand in Feb 2008, showing early signs of labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 52% of months when PAYEMS growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and retail sales jointly measure aggregate demand strength. When both GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth decelerate simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand weakness across consumption and investment, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 1.5% annualized and RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11677.1 billion in Q4 2007, showing deceleration; RSAFS at 380197 million in Feb 2008, weakening
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumption and investment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances despite joint GDPC1 and RSAFS weakness, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.
